name: Measure Extended Galaxy UV Flux
description:
An astronomer studying star formation in nearby galaxies needs to measure the total ultraviolet luminosity of an irregular galaxy disk. Calculate the background-subtracted integrated flux of the central galaxy in ~/AstroImages/raw/uit_galaxy_sample.fits using AstroImageJ. Measure the galaxy's area and integrated density using a custom region of interest tightly encompassing the galaxy, and measure the mean gray value of a source-free background region. Save the measurement results to ~/AstroImages/measurements/roi_measurements.csv. Calculate the net integrated flux using the formula: Net_Flux = Galaxy_IntDen - (Background_Mean * Galaxy_Area). Create a text file at ~/AstroImages/measurements/flux_report.txt containing the following keys with your measured and calculated numeric values:

GALAXY_AREA: <value>
GALAXY_INTDEN: <value>
BACKGROUND_MEAN: <value>
NET_FLUX: <value>
